experiences.intentions: The active piece is an O at the very top center (rows 3-4, cols 5-6 visually), and O has no meaningful rotation. The stack below is low and uneven, with a central T/L/S/I/Z structure but no immediate danger near the top. My goal now is efficient placement while preserving flexibility. Moving left or right is best to start steering the O toward a cleaner placement. The left side above the stack looks slightly more open and can help fill shallow contours without covering the center too rigidly, so I will shift left first.

experiences.intentions: Active T is high near center. Surface below has a T cavity around columns 3-5 on row 12/13, while the top is open so there is no immediate threat. Goal is to place the T efficiently and preserve a flat stack; the most promising sub-goal is to move toward the central-left notch for a future T placement rather than commit immediately. Candidate actions: hard/soft drop would place far too high on the center column and waste the T; rotations alone do not help without lateral alignment; right moves away from the useful cavity. Moving left starts aligning the T with the central notch and keeps options open for later rotation/drop.

experiences.intentions: Active T is high and centered above a mostly uneven stack with a central column occupied by a vertical I. Immediate threat is low because there is plenty of space. Goal is to position the T for a useful tuck rather than commit too early. The best near-term opportunity is moving left toward the open center-left channels; right is more obstructed by the existing right-side stack. Rotating now does not clearly improve fit, and dropping now would strand the T high on the vertical I. So shifting left preserves flexibility and advances toward a better landing zone.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is a J near the upper-left/center, with plenty of vertical space below but a relatively uneven stack in the lower half. Immediate threat is low since the piece is high and there are no near-top blockers. Goal is to place the J to improve surface continuity for upcoming Z,S,Z,S pieces; those pieces prefer flatter placements, so creating a cleaner left-side contour is useful. Candidate actions: hard_drop would commit too early from a mediocre central spawn position; soft_drop reduces flexibility without clear benefit; rotations don't yet create a clearly superior fit at the current column; moving right pushes toward the tall central I column and existing blocks, less attractive. Moving left starts shifting the J toward the more open left side where it can later be dropped/rotated into a notch or build a flatter surface.

experiences.intentions: The active piece is a Z near the top, currently spanning columns about 4-6 with open air beneath. The stack is uneven but low enough that there is no immediate danger. My goal is to place this Z to build a flatter surface and avoid creating isolated holes before the upcoming S,Z,S,J pieces. Candidate moves: rotating right makes the Z vertical and could fit future contours, but there is no urgent need to rotate at this height; hard dropping now would commit early without alignment; soft drop is reasonable only after choosing a column. Shifting right moves the Z toward the center-right where there is a visible notch around rows 18-19 that can likely accept a Z later, and it also keeps the left side less cluttered above the J structure. So the best immediate setup move is to move right.
